LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JAMES OLSEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND HIS ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION AND GRANTED AS TO THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. A SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTION IS FRAMED BY THE PLEADING - IN THIS CASE THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LOGICAL IDEAS, INC. - THAT IS CHALLENGED BY THE MOTION. PARAGRAPHS 31-38 OF LOGICAL'S COMPLAINT, WHICH ARE INCORPORATED BY REFERENCE INTO ALL THREE CAUSES OF ACTION, ALLEGE THAT MR. OLSEN IS LIABLE AS THE ALTER EGO OF DEFENDANT COASTAL VENTURES, INC. BECAUSE MR. OLSEN'S MOVING PAPERS (PARTICULARLY HIS SEPARATE STATEMENT) DO NOT SHOW THAT LOGICAL IS UNABLE TO PREVAIL ON ITS ALLEGATIONS THAT MR. OLSEN IS LIABLE AS THE ALTER EGO OF COASTAL, MR. OLSEN FAILED TO SATISFY HIS INITIAL BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT HE IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN HIS FAVOR ON ANY OF LOGICAL'S CLAIMS. MR. OLSEN IS ENTITLED TO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON LOGICAL'S PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES BECAUSE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE NOT RECOVERABLE AS A MATTER OF LAW ON ANY OF LOGICAL'S CLAIMS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE ON THE GROUNDS THAT THERE IS SOME EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THAT MR. OLSEN, AS A PARTNER, IS LIABLE ON THE CONTRACT AT ISSUE. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Derek A. Newman, Tel. No. 1-310-359-8188 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Charles Tillage of Hayes, Scott Bonino & Clause LLP, Tel. No. 1-650-637-9100
appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Logical Ideas, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted as to defendants Coastal Ventures, Inc. and Richard Chiang and the motion and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied as to defendant James Olsen. Coastal and Mr. Chiang did not oppose the motion. There are triable disputes whether Mr. Olsen is a partner, alter ego or otherwise responsible for the debts of Coastal. Mr. Olsen is not bound by Mr. Chiang's and Coastal's admissions. (CCP 2033.410(b)). Mr. Olsen presents admissible evidence that he was an independent contractor working for Coastal, did not pay and was not responsible for its debts, there was no partnership and he is not a partner of Coastal. (Olsen Depo. 83:25-84:20, 104:7-25, 108:7-109:8; Olsen Dec. pars. 3-9; Chiang Dec. pars. 4 and 7-9). Order signed in open court. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Leeor Neta of the law offices of Newman + DuWors (415.944.5422) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Charles E. Tillage of the law offices of Hayes Scott Bonino Ellingson McLay LLP (650.486.2888) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT JAMES OLSEN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED and his alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the second and third causes of action and granted as to the prayer for punitive damages. A summary judgment/adjudication motion is framed by the pleading - in this case the complaint filed by plaintiff Logical Ideas, Inc. - that is challenged by the motion. Paragraphs 31-38 of Logical's complaint, which are incorporated by reference into all three causes of action, allege that Mr. Olsen is liable as the alter ego of defendant Coastal Ventures, Inc. Because Mr. Olsen's moving papers (particularly his separate statement) do not show that Logical is unable to prevail on its allegations that Mr. Olsen is liable as the alter ego of Coastal, Mr. Olsen failed to satisfy his initial burden of showing that he is entitled to judgment in his favor on any of Logical's claims. Mr. Olsen is entitled to summary adjudication on Logical's prayer for punitive damages because punitive damages are not recoverable as a matter of law on any of Logical's claims. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org. (D302)
